top_signals.title: China Unveils Nuclear-Powered Floating Hub for Zero-Emission Shipping
top_signals.summary: China has proposed a large offshore logistics platform powered by nuclear energy that would function as both a cargo transfer hub and a refuelling/charging centre for ships, according to the South China Morning Post. The concept, unveiled by Jiangnan Shipyard, combines port infrastructure, energy generation, and cargo handling into a single floating facility aimed at reducing emissions in maritime transport. The project was presented at the Posidonia International Shipping Exhibition in Greece. The SCMP writes that the platform would rely on a…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Physical Oil Markets Are Floundering Despite 100 Days of War - Bloomberg.com
top_signals.summary: Physical Oil Markets Are Floundering Despite 100 Days of War  Bloomberg.com
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Central Asia Metals’ copper-zinc production outpaces last year
top_signals.summary: The UK-listed miner also reported significantly higher realized prices for the metals through the five-month period.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Tether leads up to $1.4 billion round in robotics firm Neura, plans crypto wallet integration
top_signals.summary: Tether will also provide and "deploy" technology within the Neura robotics ecosystem, including a crypto wallet integration.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: CFTC proposes framework favoring sports event contracts over gambling
top_signals.summary: The proposed rules would preserve election markets and allow many sports-based prediction contracts while limiting bets that could encourage manipulation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: One in three defence firms ‘can’t find graduates to hire’
top_signals.summary: Nearly one in three defence firms can’t find graduates to hire despite a youth unemployment crisis, a new report has found, as universities have been urged to work more closely with businesses.  Research by the University of Manchester and CBI Economics has suggested that skills shortages among young people has made it more difficult for [...]

top_signals.title: Making the jump to self-employment could damage your pension savings
top_signals.summary: Employees making the leap into self-employment could put their retirement at risk, with many unlikely to continue saving into a pension upon leaving auto-enrolment. Roughly one in five self-employed workers save into a private pension, compared with around four in five employees, leaving those working for themselves at greater risk of having insufficient savings upon [...]

top_signals.title: How The Macallan mastered the long game
top_signals.summary: This month’s issue of Whisky Business, City AM’s monthly look at the world of whisky, takes a look at The Macallan and its unique ownership structure. The Macallan is one of the most iconic Scotch whisky brands, having carved out a niche for itself at the luxury end of the market. Bottles have consistently broken records at [...]

top_signals.title: MP Materials COO increases shareholding
top_signals.summary: COO Michael Rosenthal bought 10,000 shares at $54.30 per share.
